Lumify
Table of Contents

Class AccountController

Namespace
lumify.api.Controllers
Assembly
lumify.api.dll

Endpoints for user accounts: login/logout, registration, two-factor authentication (TOTP), password reset and e-mail verification. Sessions are issued via an HttpOnly JWT cookie plus a CSRF double-submit cookie.

[ApiController]
[Route("account/[action]")]
public class AccountController : ControllerBase
Inheritance
object
ControllerBase
AccountController

Constructors

AccountController(ILogger<AccountController>, IConfiguration, IWebHostEnvironment, LumifyDbContext, InternalLogic, IEmailService, IOptions<AppSettings>)

Creates the controller with its injected dependencies (logging, configuration, hosting environment, database context, internal logic helpers, e-mail service and application settings).

public AccountController(ILogger<AccountController> logger, IConfiguration config, IWebHostEnvironment env, LumifyDbContext context, InternalLogic logic, IEmailService emailService, IOptions<AppSettings> appSettings)

Parameters

logger ILogger<AccountController>
config IConfiguration
env IWebHostEnvironment
context LumifyDbContext
logic InternalLogic
emailService IEmailService
appSettings IOptions<AppSettings>

Methods

ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest)

Confirms 2FA setup: a valid TOTP code flips two-factor authentication on.

[HttpPost]
[Authorize]
[ActionName("confirmTotp")]
public Task<IActionResult> ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest dto)

Parameters

dto TotpCodeRequest

The 6-digit code from the authenticator app.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with enabled = true on success; 400 if the code is missing, setup was not started, or the code is invalid; 404 if the user no longer exists.

DisableTotp(TotpCodeRequest)

Disables 2FA. Requires a valid current TOTP code so a hijacked session cannot silently turn it off.

[HttpPost]
[Authorize]
[ActionName("disableTotp")]
public Task<IActionResult> DisableTotp(TotpCodeRequest dto)

Parameters

dto TotpCodeRequest

The 6-digit code from the authenticator app.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with enabled = false on success; 400 if the code is missing, 2FA is not enabled, or the code is invalid; 404 if the user no longer exists.

LoginUser(LoginRequest)

Authenticates a user by username or e-mail and password.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("loginUser")]
public Task<IActionResult> LoginUser(LoginRequest dto)

Parameters

dto LoginRequest

Credentials: Identifier (username or e-mail) and password.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with user data (session in cookie) on success without 2FA; 200 with mfaRequired = true and an MFA token when 2FA is enabled; 401 for invalid credentials; 403 when the e-mail address is not yet confirmed.

Remarks

If 2FA is enabled, no session is issued yet — a short-lived MFA challenge token is returned instead and the client must call VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest) with the 6-digit code. Soft-deleted and unconfirmed accounts cannot log in.

LogoutUser()

Logs the user out by expiring the session and CSRF cookies immediately.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("logoutUser")]
public IActionResult LogoutUser()

Returns

IActionResult

200 once the cookies have been cleared.

MfaStatus()

Returns whether 2FA is currently active for the logged-in user.

[HttpGet]
[Authorize]
[ActionName("mfaStatus")]
public Task<IActionResult> MfaStatus()

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with enabled (bool); 404 if the user no longer exists.

RegisterUser(RegisterRequest, CancellationToken)

Registers a new user account and sends an e-mail verification link. No automatic login — the account must confirm its e-mail before it can sign in.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("registerUser")]
public Task<IActionResult> RegisterUser(RegisterRequest dto, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

dto RegisterRequest

Registration data (e-mail, username, password, optional name/avatar).

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with verificationRequired = true on success; 400 if required fields are missing; 409 if the username or e-mail already exists; 500 on error.

Remarks

An optional base64 avatar is stored; failure to save it is logged but does not abort registration (a default avatar is used instead).

RequestPasswordReset(RequestPasswordResetRequest, CancellationToken)

Password reset step 1: requests a reset link for the given username or e-mail.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("requestPasswordReset")]
public Task<IActionResult> RequestPasswordReset(RequestPasswordResetRequest dto, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

dto RequestPasswordResetRequest

The identifier (username or e-mail).

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with a generic message in all cases.

Remarks

Always returns the same generic message regardless of whether the account exists, so the endpoint cannot be used to enumerate registered users. Any previously open reset tokens for the user are invalidated; only the hash of the new token is stored.

ResendVerification(ResendVerificationRequest, CancellationToken)

Resends a verification mail for an existing, still-unconfirmed account.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("resendVerification")]
public Task<IActionResult> ResendVerification(ResendVerificationRequest dto, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

dto ResendVerificationRequest

The identifier (username or e-mail).

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with a generic message in all cases.

Remarks

Always answers with the same generic message (no user enumeration); a mail is only actually sent for an account that exists and is not yet confirmed.

ResetPassword(ResetPasswordRequest, CancellationToken)

Password reset step 2: sets a new password using the single-use token from the reset link.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("resetPassword")]
public Task<IActionResult> ResetPassword(ResetPasswordRequest dto, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

dto ResetPasswordRequest

The reset token and the new password (minimum 8 characters).

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with a success message; 400 if input is missing, the password is too short, or the token is invalid, already used, or expired.

Remarks

The token is looked up by its hash (the raw value is never stored) and consumed on success, so each reset link works only once and only before it expires.

SetupTotp()

Starts 2FA setup: generates a fresh TOTP secret and a QR code. 2FA is not active until confirmed via ConfirmTotp(TotpCodeRequest).

[HttpPost]
[Authorize]
[ActionName("setupTotp")]
public Task<IActionResult> SetupTotp()

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with secret (for manual entry) and qrCodeDataUri (for scanning); 400 if 2FA is already enabled; 404 if the user no longer exists.

VerifyEmail(VerifyEmailRequest, CancellationToken)

Confirms an account via the single-use token from the verification mail.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("verifyEmail")]
public Task<IActionResult> VerifyEmail(VerifyEmailRequest dto, CancellationToken ct)

Parameters

dto VerifyEmailRequest

The verification token.

ct CancellationToken

Cancellation token for the request.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with a success message; 400 if the token is missing, invalid, already used, or expired.

Remarks

The token is looked up by its hash and consumed on success, so the verification link works only once and only before it expires.

VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest)

Login step 2: verifies the 6-digit TOTP code for a 2FA-enabled account and then issues the real session.

[HttpPost]
[ActionName("verifyTotpLogin")]
public Task<IActionResult> VerifyTotpLogin(VerifyTotpLoginRequest dto)

Parameters

dto VerifyTotpLoginRequest

The MFA challenge token from step 1 and the 6-digit code.

Returns

Task<IActionResult>

200 with user data and the session cookie on success; 400 if token or code are missing; 401 if the MFA session or the code is invalid or expired.

WhoAmI()

Diagnostic endpoint that returns the current authentication state and the claims of the logged-in user.

[HttpGet]
[Authorize]
[ActionName("whoami")]
public IActionResult WhoAmI()

Returns

IActionResult

200 with Authenticated (bool) and the list of Claims.
